strengths: Roughly 19 years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform sustained since 2016 and used by Green Map
Consistent knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and conference talks (FOSS4G, Selenium training, testing workshops)
concerns: AI experience is shallow and very recent — only ~1 year of AI-agent tooling and MCP work at re:cinq, with no demonstrated AI products, platforms, or recognized AI expertise the role prefers

Career history is senior/staff-level delivery IC work (pricing features, data integration, product development) with no evidence of director-level scope, organizational strategy, executive advising, or influence at the level a Principal AI-strategy role demands

No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and cloud experience is incidental (AWS CDK for a pricing team, Kubernetes deploys for a side project) rather than the platform-strategy depth the role centers on; notable investment in the niche DLang ecosystem rather than mainstream cloud/AI stacks

key_factors.factor: Limited AI depth and no recognized AI expertise
Exceeds 15-year experience minimum with broad engineering range

response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile hands-on engineer who clears the raw years-of-experience bar, but the role is a director-level individual contributor charged with setting AI and cloud platform strategy, advising strategic customers, and carrying recognized AI authority. His AI exposure amounts to about a year of agent-infrastructure tooling, with no shipped AI products, publications, or public standing in the field; his cloud work is tactical rather than strategic; and his trajectory has plateaued at senior/staff delivery roles focused on execution, not organization-level technical direction. The gap between a strong senior product engineer and a principal-level AI strategist is the defining mismatch here, making him a weak fit for this position.
